name: Ants on the Hill (Chinese Style Beef)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Chicken soup stock, or Canned chicken broth
1 lb Ground chuck, sirloin or Beef stew
1 tbsp Corn, vegetable or Safflower oil
1 ts Minced fresh garlic OR 2 large cloves garlic, minced
2 tbsp Fresh scallions OR 1 Scallion with green top, diced
recipeInstructions:
Marinate:
1 ts Sugar
1/4 ts Black pepper
1/4 ts Baking soda
2 tbsp Soy sauce
1 tbsp Oyster sauce
1 tsp Sesame seed oil
1 tbsp Dry sherry (gin or vodka), optional
Binder: Mix cornstarch and chicken soup stock in a cup or small bowl until smooth. Set aside.
Marinate: Mix sugar, black pepper, baking soda, soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame seed oil, and dry sherry (if using) in a medium bowl until smooth. Add beef to marinade and blend well. Set aside.
Cooking:
1. Spread fried cellophane noodles evenly on serving platter.
2. Heat skillet or wok on high for 30 seconds. Add oil and swirl to coat the skillet for 30 seconds longer.
3. Add garlic and stir fry for 15 seconds. Add beef and marinade. Stir fry for 2 minutes, until beef loses its pink color.
4. Add binder to beef in skillet and blend well. Cook for 1 minute.
5. Spoon cooked beef evenly over noodles. Garnish with scallions sprinkled on top. Serve hot with rice and salad or vegetable of your choice. Yields 4 to 6 servings.
Noodles:
1. Heat 3 cups oil (corn, vegetable, or safflower) almost to smoking, about 375°F.
2. Unwrap one package (2 oz.) cellophane noodles and drop into the hot oil.
3. Within 3 seconds, the noodles will puff up and turn white and crunchy.
4. With a slotted spoon or wire mesh strainer, turn noodles over and deep fry for several seconds.
5. Remove from hot oil and drain on paper towels. May be stored in a tightly covered container for about a week. Makes 5 cups fried noodles.
